text: This is a seventy-something-year-old female with a little bit of adhesions from prior surgery, but acute cholecystitis admitted overnight again, stone seen on ultrasound, a little bit of pericholecystic fluid, but nothing crazy.
The surgeon performs infundibular dissection using the robotic arm equipped with a prograsp instrument, manipulating the gallbladder with precise control.
The surgeon dissects the cystic duct, with the cystic artery visible in the surgical field, providing a clear view of these structures.
The surgeon uses robotic instruments to grasp and control small branching vessels near the cystic duct, which are bleeding slightly as clips are applied.

The surgeon obtains the critical view of safety, with the liver margin visible in the background. A crossing vessel exhibits minor bleeding, which is controlled using cautery. The surgeon continues working up the cystic plate.
Pretty straightforward, especially again for a call bladder, as I call it, on call gallbladder.
